title: The Humming-Bird.
content:
A route of evanescence
    With a revolving wheel;
    A resonance of emerald,
    A rush of cochineal;
    And every blossom on the bush
    Adjusts its tumbled head, --
    The mail from Tunis, probably,
    An easy morning's ride.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Humming-Bird."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
